name: Compare
description: Compares two inputs based on a chosen operation.
python_code:
def _run_compare(self, inputs):
    def convert(val):
        if val is None: return 0
        try: return float(val)
        except (ValueError, TypeError): return str(val)

    # Use current execution inputs instead of stale instance parameters
    a = convert(inputs.get('a'))
    b = convert(inputs.get('b'))
    op = inputs.get('operation', '==')
    
    self.log_info(f"Comparing: {a} {op} {b}")
    
    try:
        if op == '==': res = a == b
        elif op == '!=': res = a != b
        elif op == '>': res = a > b
        elif op == '<': res = a < b
        elif op == '>=': res = a >= b
        elif op == '<=': res = a <= b
        else: res = False
        return res
    except Exception as e:
        self.log_error(f"Comparison error: {e}")
        return False

async def execute(self, inputs):
    res = self._run_compare(inputs)
    await self.set_output('result', res)
    await self.set_output('exec_out', True)
    return {'result': res}

async def on_parameter_changed(self, name, value):
    # This is for UI/Reactive updates, using instance parameters is correct here
    if name in ['a', 'b', 'operation']:
        def convert(val):
            if val is None: return 0
            try: return float(val)
            except (ValueError, TypeError): return str(val)
        
        a = convert(self.get_parameter('a'))
        b = convert(self.get_parameter('b'))
        op = self.get_parameter('operation', '==')
        res = False
        try:
            if op == '==': res = a == b
            elif op == '!=': res = a != b
            elif op == '>': res = a > b
            elif op == '<': res = a < b
            elif op == '>=': res = a >= b
            elif op == '<=': res = a <= b
        except: pass
        await self.set_output('result', res)
